I think it has a tarnished record regarding its taxation policy in that it has been slightly too willing to spend across the board, and I don't think we can do that anymore. I don't think the Democrats are saying that it is possible to do that anymore.

They appear more interested in social programs for social betterment - more innovative programs to help the homeless, and on health care. And a more balanced foreign policy - balanced in that it's not a knee-jerk reaction. Democrats appear to be for the Arias Program for bringing peace to that area, rather than straight aid to the Contras, while keeping the Contra option open.I believe they are more honest about difficulties to be faced in reducing the deficit - more than the Republicans.

To a degree, it is the extremity of what is favorable about them. In general, they have given tax abatements to large corporation. Steps towards a free market, which have forced the middle class to bear the brunt of the tax burden. The size of the deficit, I believe, is a direct result of a blind tendency to increase military might at the expense of domestic and economic security. The general aura of feeling is that they really don't care for those who are not making it on their own.
